title: service-accounts — field classification
description: Every credential column is `regulated_secret` (never returned on wire, never logged, never emitted to metrics). Operator-facing metadata (name, description) is `internal`. Machine-context fields (IP, user_agent) are `confidential`. Redactor rules always apply. No PII: SAs have no email, no dob, no phone. See compliance.json for SOC 2 + NIST + OWASP + PCI-DSS mapping.

regulated_secret: Plaintext exchange secret (returned ONCE at creation + rotation, then irretrievable)
JWT plaintext (issued via token-exchange, never persisted to this module's tables)

wire_projection.description: SA rows are NEVER exposed to tenant-facing routes. Only platform-admin surfaces read them. Wire projection is a redacted `ServiceAccountAdminData` DTO. Every field marked `[NEVER RETURNED]` is stripped at the serialization layer regardless of the caller's permissions.
wire_projection.tenant_wire_shape: N/A — tenant plane does not expose SA rows.

platform_admin_wire_shape.tenant_slug: computed — joined from tenants.slug when tenant_id is non-null
platform_admin_wire_shape.name: string
platform_admin_wire_shape.signer_kid: string (kid identifier; not the key material itself)

creation_wire_shape.description: The ServiceAccountCreatedResponse shape returned by POST /platform/service-accounts + POST /platform/service-accounts/{id}/rotate-secret. The `secret` field is present ONLY in this response — subsequent reads NEVER return it.

data.name: string
data.secret: string (plaintext exchange secret; base64-encoded 32 random bytes; ONE-TIME-VIEW ONLY)

never_returned: JWT signing key material (owned by auth::jwt_signing_keys; retrieved by signer_kid but the value itself is never surfaced)
Historical secret hashes (not stored — rotation overwrites the previous hash; there is no reuse-prevention history)

shape.name: string (validated: valid_service_account_name, unique per application_id)
shape.signer_kid: string | null (optional; when null, provisioner generates a fresh kid via auth module)
shape.metadata: object | null (free-form ops notes; enterprise IP-allowlist arrays go here)

input_dtos.validation_gate: Before creation, ServiceAccountObserver.creating refuses when: (a) application_id doesn't exist, (b) tenant_id (if non-null) belongs to a different application_id, (c) name is not unique within the application, (d) signer_kid (if provided) doesn't exist in auth::jwt_signing_keys or is disabled.

shape.name: string | null (validated: valid_service_account_name)
shape.expires_at: timestamp | null (may extend the current expires_at; may NOT shorten below now())

input_dtos.validation_gate: PATCH refuses to mutate application_id, tenant_id, or signer_kid. Attempting any of the three raises 422 SERVICE_ACCOUNT_IMMUTABLE_FIELD.

shape.secret: string (required; the plaintext rotation secret returned at create/rotate)
shape.requested_scope.aud: string | null (target service audience; falls back to SA metadata default)

input_dtos.validation_gate: Rate-limit checked BEFORE input parsing (per-SA + per-IP-/24). Hash::check runs constant-time bcrypt comparison. Unknown SA + wrong secret both return the same INVALID_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CREDENTIALS payload.

shape.aud: string | null (target audience; falls back to SA metadata default)

input_dtos.validation_gate: The permission platform.service_accounts.issue_test_jwt is required. TTL bounds enforced by min/max config; exceeding max raises 422 SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TTL_EXCEEDS_MAX.

output_dtos.purpose: Platform-admin listing + detail surface. See wire_projection.platform_admin_wire_shape above. Excludes secret_hash + secret plaintext.
Wraps ServiceAccountAdminData + adds the one-time-view plaintext `secret` field. Returned by POST /platform/service-accounts + POST /platform/service-accounts/{id}/rotate-secret.
Response body for the token-exchange endpoint.

Response body for GET /platform/service-accounts/{id}/audit-usage + the `service-account:audit-usage` command. Includes histogram, distinct IP-/24 count, jti-reuse detections, distinct audiences, top 100 audit-log entries in the window.

notes: SAs have NO PII. No email, no name of a human, no dob, no phone, no address. This is by construction — SAs are machines, not people.

The `last_used_ip` column IS logged unredacted to platform-admin surfaces because operator triage of anomalies genuinely needs it (IP correlation to known deployment subnets). It's classified `confidential` (not `restricted`) because there's no natural person the IP identifies — it's a machine's egress address.
There is deliberately NO password-history column. SA secrets rotate to fresh cryptographically-random values; reuse prevention is not a realistic threat (nobody memorises SA secrets).
